name: ◁ Egyptian KarkadeHIBISCUS ICED TEA
servings: prep time: 5 minutes, plus 2 hours chilling

ingredients.item: ½ cup (100 g) granulated sugar (or more per preference)
ingredients.original: ½ cup (100 g) granulated sugar (or more per preference)

ingredients.item: 1 Add the filtered water to a medium saucepan and bring to a boil.
ingredients.original: 1 Add the filtered water to a medium saucepan and bring to a boil.

ingredients.item: 2 Add the hibiscus flowers and sugar. Let boil for 5 minutes until the sugar is dissolved. Turn off the heat and allow the hibiscus to steep in the water for 20 minutes. You may taste and adjust for more sugar at this point. To add more, stir it into the tea while it is still hot until it dissolves.
ingredients.original: 2 Add the hibiscus flowers and sugar. Let boil for 5 minutes until the sugar is dissolved. Turn off the heat and allow the hibiscus to steep in the water for 20 minutes. You may taste and adjust for more sugar at this point. To add more, stir it into the tea while it is still hot until it dissolves.

ingredients.item: 3 Strain the tea into a pitcher, being careful not to spill any on easily stained counters (the hibiscus is highly pigmented).
ingredients.original: 3 Strain the tea into a pitcher, being careful not to spill any on easily stained counters (the hibiscus is highly pigmented).

ingredients.item: 4 Refrigerate for at least 2 hours. If the tea is too strong, feel free to dilute it with more cold water, but do not dilute too much if serving with ice cubes.
ingredients.original: 4 Refrigerate for at least 2 hours. If the tea is too strong, feel free to dilute it with more cold water, but do not dilute too much if serving with ice cubes.

ingredients.item: 5 Serve with ice cubes (if using) and garnish with mint leaves.
ingredients.original: 5 Serve with ice cubes (if using) and garnish with mint leaves.
